rulings.Case Title: BERTHA P PANG VS. DOUGLAS BAIRD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 28, 2019 Line 1. DEFENDANT DOUGLAS BAIRD Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement DENIED. Defendant moves for "an order enforcing the settlement in this action to compel Plaintiff to build a retaining wall." Nowhere in the Stipulation presented to the Court is there a definite obligation on the part of Plaintiff to build a wall. The parties did not come to the agreement as to "description of the process, time frames, and respective responsibilities for accomplishing the repairs." The parties agreed in relevant parts as to (1) the responsibility for payment of the costs (which has not yet ripened as no costs are alleged to have been incurred) and also agreed (2) to enter into a more detailed agreement that would include the responsibilities of the parties (presumably determining who is responsible for the "leg work" of hiring professionals, obviating permits, obtaining consent to the other side, providing notice of entry and commencement of work, etc.). These are material terms which have to be agreed upon before the Court may consider ordering a party to "build a retaining wall" pursuant to stipulation. It appears that parties attempted to reach an agreement as provided for in paragraph 7. October 18, 2017 Agreement is not enforceable in the manner sought by the Defendant. If the parties are not able to agree on the terms consistent with paragraph 7 of the Settlement Agreement, Defendant's unopposed motion for Summary Judgment (taken off calendar pursuant to paragraph 2 of the Stipulation) will be restored to calendar and will be heard on January 31, 2019 (placing the action in the same procedural position it was before the Settlement Agreement was entered into). Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $1,452.50 (fees incurred in connection with the "enforcement" of the agreement; all other fees listed were incurred in accordance with the terms of the agreement). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: COLBY LAM VS. ANDREW KENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Order Dismissing Action With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 28, 2019. Line 2. DEFENDANT M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, JAMES NUNEMACHER, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA, MICHAEL CASSIDY, BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY Notice And Motion For Order Dismissing Action With Prejudice DENIED. The Court's Order sustained the demurrer as to the alter ego-based causes of action asserted against the individual defendants. The Court did not rule on the causes of action to the extent they are based on direct liability of the Individual defendants because the demurrer did not address such allegations. "The SAC fails as against the individual defendants to the extent it is based on alter ego or agency theories of liability" See Demurrer 7:5-26. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply with this rule in the future will result in the motions being treated as unopposed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN HOM VS. PURE ENTERTAINMENT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 28, 2019 Line 7. DEFENDANT PURE ENTERTAINMENT, LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint is sufficient and not uncertain. Whether the Trust is a landlord entitled to rent under the Lease signed by Katie Hom (by operation of law or any other way) is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. = (501/CFH)
